summary: First end-to-end test of Localink. Backend pytest: 28/28 passed covering auth, guides, full booking lifecycle (create → pay → deliver itinerary → confirm → review), polling chat, role enforcement, disputes, and admin endpoints. Frontend Playwright: landing, browse with filters/search, guide profile, login-redirect-on-book, traveller register, 2-step booking flow, mock pay, booking detail, chat send/receive, local login + dashboard, itinerary delivery from local side, admin panel with tabs and stats — all working.

minor.issue: Returns 404 (not 403) when a non-owner traveller attempts to pay. Acceptable as security-by-obscurity but inconsistent with other role checks that return 403.
Accepts refund_to_traveller as query param instead of JSON body — works but stylistically inconsistent.

issues: Seed-only guard means once any test/user creates extra guides via /api/profile/guide, /browse shows >6 cards. The original spec says 6 seeded guides — there are now 9 cards because test runs created extras. Not a bug, but flag: seed_demo_data only runs when guides==0, so leftover test data persists across runs.

action_items: Optional: standardise 403 vs 404 on /bookings/{id}/pay for non-owner traveller.
Optional: move refund_to_traveller flag to JSON body for /admin/disputes/{id}/resolve.
Optional: provide an admin-side 'reseed' endpoint or test-cleanup helper since /api/profile/guide POSTs from automated tests leave permanent extra guide rows.
critical_code_review_comments: CORSMiddleware is added AFTER app.include_router(api) in server.py — works in Starlette but best practice is to add middleware before routes for clarity.
Backend is a single 686-line server.py; for maintainability consider splitting into routers (auth, guides, bookings, admin) — not blocking for MVP.
All MongoDB _id fields are correctly excluded from responses (projections {_id:0}). Good.
Password hashing uses bcrypt with gensalt — verified $2b$ format. Good.

JWT auth via Bearer header, no httpOnly cookie — matches the documented design (token in localStorage). Note this is NOT the standard /app/auth_testing.md cookie-based flow, but problem statement explicitly says custom JWT in localStorage so acceptable.
Rate limiting / brute force lockout NOT implemented on /auth/login — acceptable for MVP but worth noting.
Role enforcement is consistent and correct across all booking endpoints (verified by tests).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@localink.in / Admin@123. Demo locals: aarav.jaipur@localink.in / Local@123 (and 5 others). Test traveller registered dynamically in tests.
seed_data_creation: Tests register a fresh TEST_ traveller and TEST_ local each run; create a Delhi guide profile via the new local; create 3 bookings on Aarav's profile (one completed+reviewed, one cancelled-via-dispute, one pending). These persist in MongoDB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_localink_api.py is order-dependent (uses a session-scoped state dict). Run as a whole file, not individual tests. Email lowercasing is enforced server-side. Demo seed only runs when guides collection is empty — clear DB to re-seed exactly 6 guides. All chat polling tests work via direct API calls (no need to wait for the 4-second frontend poll on backend tests).
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing functionality. Initial pytest run had 15 failures all stemming from one root cause: backend lowercases the registration email but test assertion compared against the original-case email. Fixed by lowercasing the test email before submission.
